basic_details.job_title: ESIC Medical College Andheri Recruitment 2026: 74 Faculty & Homeopathy Physician Posts, Walk-in Interview Dates
basic_details.organization_name: ESIC Medical College and Hospital, Andheri East, Mumbai (Employees' State Insurance Corporation, Ministry of Labour & Employment, Govt. of India)
basic_details.total_vacancies: Professor, Associate Professor, Assistant Professor, Senior Resident, Homeopathy Physician
basic_details.short_information: ESIC Medical College and Hospital, Andheri East, Mumbai has released Advertisement No. 11/2026 to fill 73 contract faculty/Senior Resident posts (19 Professor, 22 Associate Professor, 11 Assistant Professor, 21 Senior Resident) across 28 departments, plus 1 Homeopathy Physician post, through a walk-in (offline) interview scheduled between 24 and 26 July 2026. Candidates must report between 9:30 AM and 2:30 PM at the Dean Office, 5th Floor, ESIC Medical College & Hospital, MIDC, Andheri East, Mumbai, with the original application form, supporting documents, and demand draft for the fee.

important_dates.24.07.2026 (friday): Anatomy, Physiology, Biochemistry, Pharmacology, Community Medicine, Forensic Medicine, Medicine, Dermatology
important_dates.25.07.2026 (saturday): Psychiatry, General Surgery, Orthopaedics, Ophthalmology, Anaesthesiology, Paediatrics, Radiology, Accident & Emergency, Homeopathy (Homeopathy Physician post)
important_dates.26.07.2026 (sunday): Burns & Plastic Surgery, Cardiology, Endocrinology & Metabolism, Gastroenterology, Medical Oncology, Nephrology, Neurology, Paediatrics Surgery, Urology, Blood Bank, Haematology, ICU

application_fee.details: The application fee differs by category and is separate for the Homeopathy Physician post compared to the faculty/Senior Resident posts, though both are payable via Demand Draft in favour of the same ESI Fund account. | The fee must be paid via Demand Draft in favour of ESI Fund Account No. 1, payable at Mumbai. Fee once paid will not be refunded under any circumstances.

age_limit.age_details: Contract basis, 1 year extendable up to 3 years subject to satisfactory performance, or till regular faculty joins/attainment of 68 years, whichever is earlier
age_limit.details: The maximum age differs by post type, with additional relaxation available only for the Homeopathy Physician post. All age limits are calculated as on 30.06.2026 and verified again on the date of interview. | Professor/Associate Professor/Assistant Professor (faculty posts): Not exceeding 68 years as on the date of interview. Senior Resident: Not exceeding 45 years as on the date of interview. Homeopathy Physician: Not exceeding 35 years as on the date of interview, with age relaxation of 3 years for OBC candidates and 5 years for SC/ST candidates. | The maximum tenure of engagement for teaching faculty will be till 70 years of age, or 1 year extendable up to 3 years subject to satisfactory performance, whichever is earlier.

qualification.details: Eligibility for these posts is based on updated medical registration, post-MBBS qualifications, and experience as per the latest NMC norms, with different requirements for faculty posts, Senior Resident, and the Homeopathy Physician post. | Educational Qualification: As per the latest NMC Gazette notification dated 30.06.2026 for all advertised posts. Experience Criteria: As per the latest NMC Gazette notification dated 30.06.2025 for all advertised posts. All candidates must have updated Medical Registration (NMC/State Medical Council) along with all post-MBBS/MD/MS qualifications. If a candidate does not have updated registration, they must submit a copy of the "applied for" document at the time of interview; failing this, they may not be interviewed, or their result may be withheld even if allowed to appear. Even after selection, candidates must submit an updated Medical Registration Certificate at the time of joining. Candidates working in Government/Autonomous/Statutory institutions should preferably carry a No Objection Certificate (NOC) at the time of interview, or a copy of the "applied for NOC" if not immediately available. | BHMS from a recognized university; preference will be given to candidates with a PG qualification. Experience certificate, if any, should be from a Government institution. Must be registered with the Maharashtra Medical Council or the Medical Council of India. Candidates registered in another state must register with the Maharashtra Medical Council at the time of joining.

salary_details.details: Faculty and Senior Resident posts follow the 7th CPC pay matrix, while the Homeopathy Physician post carries a fixed consolidated monthly amount. Here are the confirmed pay details from the notification. | Other allowances include DA, NPA, TA, HRA, etc. For the Homeopathy Physician post, the emolument is Rs. 50,000 per month for 5 hours a day, 6 days a week, with no other allowance in any form admissible. Retired employees/pensioners engaged under this notification may draw their total pay without deduction, as this is not treated as re-employment under the CCS (Fixation of Pay of Re-employed Pensioner) Orders, 1986, and they can continue to draw pension and dearness relief during the contract period.

selection_process: Selection for these posts is based on a combination of academic credentials and interview performance
followed by an offer of appointment sent only through email. Here is the process as described in the notification.
Candidates report for the walk-in interview at the scheduled date and time with the original application form

and demand draft (if fee is applicable). The selection board evaluates candidates based on their acquired academic credentials and interview performance. Results are displayed in the Recruitment section of www.esic.gov.in. The Offer of Appointment is sent only through email. Selected candidates must join as per the instructions in the offer of appointment; if a selected candidate does not join within the prescribed time
the offer is issued to the waitlisted candidate.
Mere submission of the application does not confer any right to be interviewed unless the candidate reports on the scheduled date and time with the required documents
subject to detailed scrutiny of eligibility.
how_to_apply: This is a walk-in recruitment process, meaning candidates apply by physically appearing for the interview with their application form and documents rather than submitting online. Here are the steps to follow.
Prepare the application in the prescribed format, submitted separately for each post applied for; applications in any other format or incomplete applications will be summarily rejected.
Attach self-attested copies of all required documents listed above.
Prepare the original Demand Draft for the applicable interview fee, in favour of ESI Fund Account No. 1, payable at Mumbai.

Report to the Dean Office, 5th Floor, ESIC Medical College & Hospital, MIDC, Opposite MIDC Police Station, Andheri East, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400093, between 9:30 AM and 2:30 PM on the scheduled interview date for your department.
Carry the original application form along with supporting documents and the original Demand Draft for verification.
Check www.esic.gov.in/recruitments for the final result, including the waiting list.

content_sections.heading: ESIC Medical College Andheri Recruitment 2026 - Quick Overview

rows.information: ESIC Medical College and Hospital, Andheri East, Mumbai (Employees' State Insurance Corporation, Ministry of Labour & Employment, Govt. of India)
Professor, Associate Professor, Assistant Professor, Senior Resident, Homeopathy Physician
74 (19 Professor + 22 Associate Professor + 11 Assistant Professor + 21 Senior Resident + 1 Homeopathy Physician)
Contract basis, 1 year extendable up to 3 years subject to satisfactory performance, or till regular faculty joins/attainment of 68 years, whichever is earlier
Dean Office, 5th Floor, ESIC Medical College & Hospital, MIDC, Opposite MIDC Police Station, Andheri East, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400093

content_sections.heading: Important Dates: Department-wise Interview Schedule
content_sections.text: The interview for Professor, Associate Professor, Assistant Professor, and Senior Resident posts is spread across three days based on department, while the Homeopathy Physician interview falls on the second day. Candidates should check their department against the schedule below.

rows.departments_scheduled: Anatomy, Physiology, Biochemistry, Pharmacology, Community Medicine, Forensic Medicine, Medicine, Dermatology
Psychiatry, General Surgery, Orthopaedics, Ophthalmology, Anaesthesiology, Paediatrics, Radiology, Accident & Emergency, Homeopathy (Homeopathy Physician post)
Burns & Plastic Surgery, Cardiology, Endocrinology & Metabolism, Gastroenterology, Medical Oncology, Nephrology, Neurology, Paediatrics Surgery, Urology, Blood Bank, Haematology, ICU

content_sections.heading: Vacancy Details: Department-wise and Category-wise Breakdown
content_sections.text: The 73 faculty/Senior Resident vacancies are distributed across 28 departments, with category-wise reservation (UR, OBC, SC, ST, EWS) applied within each post type. The table below lists the total vacancies for each post type, and candidates can refer to the full department breakdown in the official notification for their specific department's category allocation.

content_sections.heading: Eligibility Criteria: Qualification and Experience
content_sections.text: Eligibility for these posts is based on updated medical registration, post-MBBS qualifications, and experience as per the latest NMC norms, with different requirements for faculty posts, Senior Resident, and the Homeopathy Physician post. Educational Qualification: As per the latest NMC Gazette notification dated 30.06.2026 for all advertised posts. Experience Criteria: As per the latest NMC Gazette notification dated 30.06.2025 for all advertised posts. All candidates must have updated Medical Registration (NMC/State Medical Council) along with all post-MBBS/MD/MS qualifications. If a candidate does not have updated registration, they must submit a copy of the "applied for" document at the time of interview; failing this, they may not be interviewed, or their result may be withheld even if allowed to appear. Even after selection, candidates must submit an updated Medical Registration Certificate at the time of joining . Candidates working in Government/Autonomous/Statutory institutions should preferably carry a No Objection Certificate (NOC) at the time of interview, or a copy of the "applied for NOC" if not immediately available. BHMS from a recognized university ; preference will be given to candidates with a PG qualification. Experience certificate, if any, should be from a Government institution. Must be registered with the Maharashtra Medical Council or the Medical Council of India . Candidates registered in another state must register with the Maharashtra Medical Council at the time of joining.

content_sections.heading: Age Limit
content_sections.text: The maximum age differs by post type, with additional relaxation available only for the Homeopathy Physician post. All age limits are calculated as on 30.06.2026 and verified again on the date of interview. Professor/Associate Professor/Assistant Professor (faculty posts): Not exceeding 68 years as on the date of interview. Senior Resident: Not exceeding 45 years as on the date of interview. Homeopathy Physician: Not exceeding 35 years as on the date of interview, with age relaxation of 3 years for OBC candidates and 5 years for SC/ST candidates . The maximum tenure of engagement for teaching faculty will be till 70 years of age , or 1 year extendable up to 3 years subject to satisfactory performance, whichever is earlier.

content_sections.heading: Salary and Pay Scale
content_sections.text: Faculty and Senior Resident posts follow the 7th CPC pay matrix, while the Homeopathy Physician post carries a fixed consolidated monthly amount. Here are the confirmed pay details from the notification. Other allowances include DA, NPA, TA, HRA, etc. For the Homeopathy Physician post, the emolument is Rs. 50,000 per month for 5 hours a day, 6 days a week, with no other allowance in any form admissible . Retired employees/pensioners engaged under this notification may draw their total pay without deduction, as this is not treated as re-employment under the CCS (Fixation of Pay of Re-employed Pensioner) Orders, 1986, and they can continue to draw pension and dearness relief during the contract period.

content_sections.heading: Application Fee
content_sections.text: The application fee differs by category and is separate for the Homeopathy Physician post compared to the faculty/Senior Resident posts, though both are payable via Demand Draft in favour of the same ESI Fund account. The fee must be paid via Demand Draft in favour of ESI Fund Account No. 1, payable at Mumbai . Fee once paid will not be refunded under any circumstances .

tables.caption: For Professor, Associate Professor, Assistant Professor and Senior Resident

rows.category: ESIC (Regular Employee), Female Candidate, Defence Ex-servicemen, PH candidate

content_sections.heading: Selection Process
content_sections.text: Selection for these posts is based on a combination of academic credentials and interview performance, followed by an offer of appointment sent only through email. Here is the process as described in the notification. Candidates report for the walk-in interview at the scheduled date and time with the original application form, supporting documents, and demand draft (if fee is applicable). The selection board evaluates candidates based on their acquired academic credentials and interview performance . Results are displayed in the Recruitment section of www.esic.gov.in . The Offer of Appointment is sent only through email . Selected candidates must join as per the instructions in the offer of appointment; if a selected candidate does not join within the prescribed time, the offer is issued to the waitlisted candidate . Mere submission of the application does not confer any right to be interviewed unless the candidate reports on the scheduled date and time with the required documents, and interview opportunities remain provisional, subject to detailed scrutiny of eligibility.

content_sections.heading: Required Documents
content_sections.text: Candidates must attach self-attested copies of several documents with the application form, which will later be verified against originals before the interview or before an offer of appointment is generated. Certificate of passing Class 10th, for Date of Birth proof All marksheets, attempt records, and degree certificate of MBBS All marksheets, attempt records, and degree certificate of MD/MS/DNB/other examination Valid EWS/OBC/SC/ST/PwD certificate, issued in the format applicable for Central Government jobs, wherever applicable State/NMC Medical Registration Certificate (updated) Proof of publication(s) Experience certificate Recent passport-size photograph of the candidate Relieving/NOC certificate from previous/current employer, if applicable Aadhaar Card Completion certificate of ICMR/NMC-approved basic course in Biomedical Research, if any NMC-approved revised basic course workshop/Basic Course in Medical Education workshop certificate, if any

content_sections.heading: How to Apply
content_sections.text: This is a walk-in recruitment process, meaning candidates apply by physically appearing for the interview with their application form and documents rather than submitting online. Here are the steps to follow. Prepare the application in the prescribed format , submitted separately for each post applied for; applications in any other format or incomplete applications will be summarily rejected. Attach self-attested copies of all required documents listed above. Prepare the original Demand Draft for the applicable interview fee, in favour of ESI Fund Account No. 1, payable at Mumbai. Report to the Dean Office, 5th Floor, ESIC Medical College & Hospital, MIDC, Opposite MIDC Police Station, Andheri East, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400093 , between 9:30 AM and 2:30 PM on the scheduled interview date for your department. Carry the original application form along with supporting documents and the original Demand Draft for verification. Check www.esic.gov.in/recruitments for the final result, including the waiting list.

content_sections.heading: Terms of Contract
content_sections.text: These are contractual engagements with specific conditions on tenure, termination, and service benefits that differ from a regular government job. Candidates should review these carefully before applying. The tenure for all posts is 1 year, extendable up to 3 years subject to satisfactory performance on a contract basis, or attainment of 68 years of age, whichever is earlier. The engagement can be terminated or discontinued from either side with one month's prior notice , without assigning any reason. In case of spot resignation, one month's salary must be deposited to the ESI account through challan. No claim for service benefits such as PF, Pension, Gratuity, Medical Allowance, Medical Benefits, Seniority, or Promotion will be admissible. Being a contractual appointment, absence from work for more than 7 days without proper permission from the competent authority will be treated as voluntary abandonment of engagement and result in automatic termination. Teaching faculty are required to mark attendance manually as well as on the Aadhaar Enabled Bio-metric Attendance System (AEBAS) daily on scheduled working days. No private practice is allowed in any capacity after joining as teaching faculty or Senior Resident at ESIC Medical College & Hospital, Andheri East, Mumbai. No TA/DA will be paid to candidates for appearing in the interview or for submission of any document.

faq.question: Q1. What are the interview dates for ESIC Medical College Andheri Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The walk-in interviews are scheduled on 24, 25, and 26 July 2026, depending on the department.

faq.question: Q2. How many total vacancies are there in ESIC Advertisement No. 11/2026?
faq.answer: There are 74 total vacancies - 19 Professor, 22 Associate Professor, 11 Assistant Professor, 21 Senior Resident, and 1 Homeopathy Physician.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for faculty and Senior Resident posts at ESIC Andheri?
faq.answer: Faculty posts (Professor/Associate Professor/Assistant Professor) have a maximum age of 68 years, while Senior Resident has a maximum age of 45 years, both as on the date of interview.

faq.question: Q4. What is the salary for a Professor at ESIC Medical College Andheri?
faq.answer: A Professor is placed at Pay Level 13, Index-1, with a Basic Pay of Rs. 1,23,100 and an approximate total emolument of Rs. 2,81,704 including allowances.

faq.question: Q5. What is the eligibility for the Homeopathy Physician post?
faq.answer: Candidates need a BHMS degree from a recognized university and must be registered with the Maharashtra Medical Council or Medical Council of India, with preference given to candidates holding a PG qualification.

faq.question: Q6. What is the application fee for this ESIC recruitment?
faq.answer: For faculty/Senior Resident posts, the fee is Rs. 300 for UR/OBC and Rs. 125 for SC/ST, with no fee for ESIC regular employees, female candidates, ex-servicemen, and PH candidates; for Homeopathy Physician, it is the same structure with no fee for women and PWD candidates.

faq.question: Q7. Is this recruitment for permanent posts at ESIC?
faq.answer: No, these are contractual posts for 1 year, extendable up to 3 years subject to satisfactory performance, or until a regular faculty joins or the candidate reaches 68 years of age, whichever is earlier.

faq.question: Q8. How can candidates apply for ESIC Medical College Andheri Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: This is a walk-in interview process - candidates must report in person with the original application form, documents, and demand draft to the Dean Office at the scheduled date and time for their department.
